page: Amazon Web Services m7a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 128 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor, which operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or discrete GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7a.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, particularly in stress-ng multi-core tests and PassMark CPU suites, including physics and encryption workloads. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, reaching 550.79 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Software compilation via Clang and ray tracing benchmarks also register in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core performance is strong, as evidenced by Geekbench and stress-ng single-core metrics, though single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression perform closer to the historical average.

Given its high core density and memory bandwidth, the m7a.32xlarge is highly efficient for large-scale parallel processing tasks that do not require local storage. It excels in Redis in-memory database operations, achieving 251.75 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput. The instance is also well-suited for CPU-based machine learning tasks, showing top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLM models, though text generation speeds are more moderate. Tradeoffs include the lack of local SSD storage, meaning all disk I/O must traverse the network, which may impact latency-sensitive database workloads compared to storage-optimized instances.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory, delivering a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the m7a.32xlarge exhibits top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high throughput for Redis operations and static web serving. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM prompt processing. This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive general-purpose workloads, software compilation, in-memory caching, and parallel processing tasks that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.32xlarge features 128 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, dedicated 128-core AMD EPYC platform with DDR5 memory, engineered for demanding multi-threaded workloads and high-throughput network applications.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 4 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for software compilation, Redis, and static web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
